So a big part of our plan this year is signals versus noise, which some of you will be aware of already. But really that's about my own challenges as well. ## [09:11 - 12:12]

if I just want to look at high level as an owner or a manager, how is that at account performing? I can look at ROAS, I can look at it by day and things like that. And all of a sudden, you're prioritizing this is the most important thing to do. And as a business owner and both as a manager as well, you need to have the ability to decide what to do. So you might say, right, there's an analysis task that needs doing, I'm going to do it. I've got the time. I'm going to, you know, park something else and that's fine. But maybe in some instances, you need to delegate. And this is a skill of an effective manager is to be able to identify and say, Hey, Mr. Media Buyer, notice that the CPA was higher than it should be, had a quick look conversion rate has dropped over the last three days. Can you go and do some analysis? Tell me why is it frequency? Is it ad creative? All that kind of stuff. And then you can delegate that out. Or you say, Well, actually CPA has gone up, but conversion rates dropped today over the last three days, it's been great. We're going to park the analysis right now, we're going to revisit it in a few days time. And, you know, when you're doing that kind of conscious decision making in your Mediavine, you avoid the problem of analysis paralysis, where you're going deep into an analysis. Sometimes there isn't actually a problem to solve because, you know, maybe conversion rate has dropped today, maybe it's an auction problem, you just need to monitor it. Maybe you need to delegate that to analysis to the Mediavine and say, Can you just keep an eye on this campaign that there seems to be a drop in CPA? Maybe there's a conversion rate problem.
